findings.description: monthly_revenue_report reconstructs revenue as unit_price*quantity (no discount, no tax) so it never matches the amount actually charged via compute_order_total, which applies both — revenue figures are systematically wrong.

Per-line round() of the discounted amount before summing (and no final round before tax) causes order-dependent cent drift versus rounding the order total once, so captured cents can diverge from an independently computed total.

A zero-total order (empty items or 100% discount) yields amount_cents==0, which the gateway rejects as 'non-positive amount', so legitimately-zero-owed orders can never be marked paid and return a misleading failure.
discount_pct is never range-validated; a value >100 produces a negative line/subtotal/amount_cents and a silent gateway failure, and negative quantity/unit_price silently corrupts the total.

unit_price/quantity are used directly from the DB row with no null/type guard; a NULL column surfaces as None and raises an unhandled TypeError in the multiplication, aborting the charge instead of returning a ChargeResult error.

Partial-failure window: if gateway.charge succeeds but the subsequent UPDATE/commit throws (DB locked, disk full), the order stays unpaid while the card was charged, with no compensating action and no idempotency key, enabling double-charge on retry.
refund() flips status to 'refunded' but never calls the gateway to reverse the charge and ignores the stored tx_id, so the boolean return falsely implies money was returned.
order['discount_pct'] with 'or 0.0' guards NULL/0 but not an absent column; if the schema lacks discount_pct the Row access raises KeyError/IndexError rather than defaulting.
tx_id is selected in monthly_revenue_report but never used, and the actually-charged amount is never persisted, so the report has no source of truth to reconcile revenue against captured payments.
logger.info writes the full card number and CVV in plaintext (and CVV must never be stored/logged per PCI-DSS) — flagged in passing; defer detail to critic-security.
monthly_revenue_report issues one order_items query per order (N+1); could be a single JOIN/GROUP BY — flagged in passing; defer detail to critic-performance.
